# Grid Plugins — Catalog (25 plugins + shell)

> System-level plugin architecture (manager, lifecycle, hooks, manifest, communication, scroll, incompatibility) lives in grid-plugins.md. This file is the per-plugin OWNS/HOOKS/DECIDED catalogue.

## all-plugins (25 total, categorized)

### Row-Transforming (modifiesRowStructure: true)

**ServerSide** — OWNS: fetch cache, lazy blocks, placeholder nodes (`{__loading:true,__index}`), `managedNodes`, `blockControllers: Map<number,AbortController>`. HOOKS: processRows(-10) — IGNORES input, returns `managedNodes` (length-clamped to viewport). EVENTS: datasource:data/children/loading/error. QUERIES: datasource:fetch-children, datasource:is-active. LISTENS: sort-change, filter-change.

- INVARIANT: `totalNodeCount=-1` activates infinite scroll; `0` = unknown; short block auto-detects end. `grid.sourceRows` stays empty. `onModelChange` MUST call `loadRequiredBlocks()` after cache clear. Block resolution with `previousManagedLength === 0` OR `managedNodes.length < totalNodeCount` MUST `requestRender()` ROWS (not `requestVirtualRefresh()` — skips processRows). `loadRequiredBlocks()` expands viewport by `loadThreshold` rows both dirs before computing blocks.
- DECIDED (Apr 2026): core `applySort`/`grid.sort()` emits `sort-change` to BOTH DOM and plugin bus via `_pluginManager.emitPluginEvent`. WHY: ServerSide otherwise misses without MultiSort. `setDataSource()` calls `loadRequiredBlocks()` after initial block only when `loadThreshold > 0` (else breaks ~12 tests; preserves "first fetch = block 0").
- DECIDED (Apr 2026, AbortSignal + Subscribable): `getRows()` receives non-aborted `AbortSignal` on `params.signal` (REQUIRED). Return `Promise<GetRowsResult>` OR `Subscribable<GetRowsResult>` (duck-typed `{subscribe(observer):{unsubscribe()}}` — no RxJS dep). Per-block `AbortController` aborted on `setDataSource`/`refresh`/`purgeCache`/`onModelChange`/`detach`. `toResultPromise`: Subscribables subscribe once, settle on `next`, unsubscribe on error/complete/abort (cancels Angular HttpClient XHR without `firstValueFrom`/`takeUntil`). Promise sources defensively rejected with `DOMException('Aborted','AbortError')`. Result-shape guard drops stored result if abort lands before `loadedBlocks.set`. `setDataSource()` adds block 0 to `loadingBlocks` (parallel scroll-triggered call won't double-fetch). RULED OUT: separate `fromObservable` adapter helper (polymorphic core ~30 LoC cleaner). Files: `server-side/datasource.ts`, `ServerSidePlugin.ts` (`abortAllBlocks`). Tests: `server-side.spec.ts` (AbortSignal + Subscribable).
- DECIDED (Feb 2026, pageSize canonical): `ServerSideConfig.pageSize` documented; `cacheBlockSize` `@deprecated` alias. Resolve at consumption: `this.config.pageSize ?? this.config.cacheBlockSize ?? 100`. `defaultConfig` MUST NOT set `pageSize` (would outrank legacy specs setting only `cacheBlockSize`). `GetRowsParams.pageSize: number` (= `endNode - startNode`) for REST `?start=0&pageSize=100`. Test: `server-side.spec.ts > loadBlock`.
- DECIDED (Jun 2026, #273, declarative `data-src` host attr): plugins may read host-level `data-*` attributes off the `<tbw-grid>` element in `attach()` via `this.gridElement.getAttribute('data-*')`. ServerSide is the proof: when no JS `config.dataSource`, `attach()` reads `data-src` and calls `setDataSource(createUrlDataSource(src))`. `createUrlDataSource(url)` (server-side/datasource.ts, `@internal`) = fetch-once-on-success-and-cache: fetches the URL with `params.signal`, accepts a plain array (`totalNodeCount=length`) OR `{rows,totalNodeCount}` envelope (honours `totalNodeCount`), then slices per `getRows` block from the cached dataset. A failed/aborted fetch is NOT cached (`datasetPromise=null` in `.catch`) — otherwise the rejected promise would poison every later block load (plugin aborts in-flight blocks on sort/filter/refresh via `abortAllBlocks()`), leaving the grid blank. Enables a zero-JS server-fetched grid from static HTML. INVARIANT: host `data-*` read is INIT-TIME ONLY — `observedAttributes` is a static class getter (`['rows','columns','grid-config','fit-mode','loading']`) so per-plugin host attrs CANNOT be reactive without a per-plugin MutationObserver; the JSON `grid-config` attr / JS property remain the reactive escape hatch. `data-*` namespace avoids colliding with core reactive attrs. JS `config.dataSource` ALWAYS wins over `data-src`. Zero core-bundle delta (lives in server-side plugin chunk). NOT for true server pagination/remote sort-filter — use a `dataSource` callback. Tests: `server-side.spec.ts > createUrlDataSource` + `> data-src host attribute (declarative)`. FOLLOW-UP (#369 review): `FeatureConfig.serverSide` widened `ServerSideConfig` → `boolean | ServerSideConfig` so `features:{serverSide:true}` is type-valid (was the lone object-only feature among 18 `boolean|XxxConfig` siblings); factory normalizes `if (config===true) new ServerSidePlugin()`. This is what makes the zero-JS `data-src` example (`{"features":{"serverSide":true}}`) compile. NOTE: spreading a boolean in `BaseGridPlugin` (`{...true}`) yields `{}`, does NOT throw (registry treats any truthy feature value as enabled). Test: `server-side.spec.ts > serverSide feature boolean shorthand`.
- TENSION: tall grid + small `cacheBlockSize` + no threshold still needs scroll to fill viewport.

**Tree** — OWNS: expanded keys, flattened rows, row key map (`Map<key,FlattenedTreeRow>`), per-row metadata (`WeakMap<row,FlattenedTreeRow>`), per-row stable keys (`WeakMap<row,string>`), animation state, loading keys. HOOKS: processRows(10), processColumns, afterCellRender, onCellClick, onHeaderClick, renderRow, getRowHeight, adjustVirtualStart. QUERIES: canMoveRow, datasource:viewport-mapping, sort:get-model. EVENTS: tree-expand. FIRES: datasource:fetch-children (on expand of lazy nodes).

- INVARIANT: lazy children signaled by truthy non-array childrenField value (e.g. `children: true`); child rows are single-batch (no pagination).
- INVARIANT (row-identity): `processRows` returns user's source row references — never spread/clones. Decoration (depth, key, hasChildren, isExpanded) lives in `#rowMeta` WeakMap keyed by row identity; renderer reads via `getRowMeta(row)`. Stable keys live in `#rowKeys` WeakMap. Path-based keys assigned in _unsorted_ order before `#sortLevel` so they match `expandAll`'s key generation.

- DECIDED (Apr 2026, row-identity — ALL row-model plugins): plugins MUST NOT spread/clone row objects in `processRows`. Output is either (a) `===` input refs, or (b) genuinely synthetic (group headers, pivot aggregates, ServerSide `__loading`/`managedNodes`). Decoration goes in parallel structures — `WeakMap<row,meta>` or wrappers (GroupingRows `RenderRow{kind:'data',row}`). WHY: `RowManager.updateRow` mutates `_rows[i]` in place; next ROWS rebuild re-runs `processRows` — clones discard the mutation. Symptoms: `cell-change.row` not `===` source; mutations vanish after filter; edit dirty resets. Audit: `expect(grid._rows[i]).toBe(sourceRows[i])`. Refs: `TreePlugin.ts` `#rowKeys`/`#rowMeta`; `tree-row-update.spec.ts`.
- DECIDED (Apr 2026, sortHandler dispatch — data-row sorts): plugins sorting USER DATA ROWS MUST resolve through `gridConfig.sortHandler ?? builtInSort` (mirrors core `applySort`). Async handlers can't be awaited in sync `processRows` → fall back to `builtInSort`. `builtInSort` honors per-column `sortComparator` + `valueAccessor`. EXCEPTIONS: GroupingRows (group nodes by agg value), Pivot (row-keys), clipboard/context-menu/excel-styles (indices). Rules: (1) pass shallow copy `[...rows]` when input could be user-owned (`TreePlugin#sortLevel` per-level `row.children`). ServerSide exempt (`managedNodes` is plugin-owned). (2) Promise-returning handler: `void result.catch(()=>undefined)` BEFORE returning sync fallback (else unhandled rejection). Do NOT splice resolved Promise back into `managedNodes` async (race with next `processRows`). Refs: `TreePlugin.ts#sortLevel`, `ServerSidePlugin.ts._localSort`. PENDING: MultiSort `sortRowsInPlace` ignores `sortHandler` (signature mismatch).
- DECIDED (Apr 2026, public sort API): `column.sortComparator` RECOMMENDED. `gridConfig.sortHandler` escape hatch only — bypassed by MultiSort, sees one field at a time. Server-side: `ServerSidePlugin` `dataSource.getRows({sortModel})`. WHY: `sortComparator` survives every sort path (core/multi-sort/tree/server-side `sortMode:'local'`) and composes. Doc steer: `core/types.ts` JSDoc, `apps/docs/.../multi-sort/index.mdx`.
- DECIDED (May 2026, #264 WAI-ARIA Treegrid): Tree or GroupingRows registered → swap `.rows-body` `role` from `grid` to `treegrid` + emit `aria-level`/`aria-setsize`/`aria-posinset` per visible row. WHY: `aria-expanded` on hierarchical rows only valid inside `treegrid`; SRs need "level 2, item 3 of 5". Both set role idempotently in `afterRender`, restore `role="grid"` in `detach()`. Tree: `posInSet`/`setSize` on `FlattenedTreeRow` (`tree-data.ts#flattenTree`, `TreePlugin.#flattenWithSort`). GroupingRows: parallel `flatMeta` array (`computeFlatMeta`) by flat index. `aria-multiselectable` valid on `treegrid` (SelectionPlugin unchanged). INVARIANT: `posInSet`/`setSize` is 1-based per parent (NOT global rowIndex). Tests: `tree-integration.spec.ts`, `grouping-rows-integration.spec.ts`, `tree-datasource.spec.ts#makeFlatRow`.

**GroupingRows** — OWNS: grouped row model, expanded keys, animation state. HOOKS: processRows(10), onHeaderClick(-1), renderRow. QUERIES: canMoveRow, grouping:get-grouped-fields, datasource:viewport-mapping. EVENTS: group-toggle/expand/collapse.

- DECIDED (May 2026, #335 deferred-expansion): `setGroupOn(fn, expanded?)` accepts `DefaultExpandedValue: boolean|number|string|string[]` seeding expansion against NEW group set on next rebuild. `expandAll`/`collapseAll` called immediately after `setGroupOn(fn)` ALSO defer (same `pendingExpansion` field) instead of reading stale `flattenedRows`. WHY: `setGroupOn` mutates config + `requestRender()`; `flattenedRows` rebuilds async in `processRows` — pre-fix `expandAll()` populated `expandedKeys` from previous grouping → new groups landed collapsed. Mechanism: `setGroupOn` sets `groupConfigDirty=true`; `expandAll`/`collapseAll` check flag, store `pendingExpansion` instead of touching `flattenedRows`; `processRows` snapshots+clears at top, resolves against fresh `getGroupKeys(initialBuild)`, applies unconditionally, broadcasts `group-toggle` once.
- INVARIANT: bulk `group-toggle` emissions (deferred-apply, `expandAll`, `collapseAll`) MUST use `broadcast<GroupToggleDetail>` not `emitPluginEvent` so DOM listeners see the change. `GroupToggleDetail.{key,expanded,value,depth}` are optional (bulk ops carry only `expandedKeys`). Tests: `grouping-rows-plugin.spec.ts` (5 cases for #335).

**Pivot** — OWNS: pivot result, flattened pivot rows, expanded keys, column totals, sort state. HOOKS: onHeaderClick(-10), processRows(100). QUERIES: sort:get-sort-config. EVENTS: pivot-toggle, pivot-config-change. INVARIANT: `PivotRow.isGroup` means "has sub-groups" (`remainingFields.length > 0`), NOT "is a group row" — single `rowGroupFields` produces `isGroup: false`; `getAllGroupKeys()` returns nothing for single-level pivots

### Column-Transforming

**PinnedColumns** — OWNS: pinned state per column. HOOKS: processColumns(-10), afterCellRender. TENSION: runs first to reorder before ColumnVirtualization.

- INVARIANT (sticky-cell painting): `.sticky-left`/`.sticky-right` carry opaque `background: var(--tbw-color-panel-bg)` (`core/styles/rows.css`) + `position: sticky; z-index: 25` (`core/styles/base.css`). Two consequences for row-level visuals:
  1. **Background tints** on `.data-grid-row` (`.row-focus`, `:hover`, `:nth-child(even)`) are HIDDEN under sticky cells. Re-paint with layered gradient: `background: linear-gradient(<tint>,<tint>), var(--tbw-color-panel-bg)`.
  2. **`::after` border overlays** are covered when `z-index ≤ 25`. Fix: bump row-level `::after` to `z-index: 26` (cell-level `::after { inset: 0 }` is clipped by `overflow: hidden`). Single overlay spans pinned + unpinned with perfect alignment. Canonical: `selection.css` `.data-grid-row.row-focus`.

**ColumnVirtualization** — OWNS: visible column subset based on scroll. HOOKS: processColumns

**Visibility** — OWNS: hidden column set. HOOKS: processColumns

**GroupingColumns** — OWNS: column groups structure. HOOKS: processColumns

### Selection & Navigation

**Selection** — OWNS: selected rows/cells, ranges, columns (Set\<field\>), activeAxis, mode (normalized). HOOKS: onCellClick, onRowClick, onHeaderClick, onKeyDown, afterCellRender, processColumns (checkbox column), afterRender, onScrollRender. EVENTS: selection-change. MODES: cell, row, range, column. Mode can be `SelectionMode | SelectionMode[]` — array form must contain `'column'` plus exactly one in-row axis.

- DECIDED (Feb 2026, #269, modes + storage + chord): `mode: SelectionMode | SelectionMode[]` enables column axis alongside in-row. Normalized at `attach()` into `NormalizedModeConfig { primary, columnEnabled, bothAxes }`. Validation throws on: empty, 3+ items, 2-element without `'column'`, duplicates, unknown. RULED OUT: `['cell','row']` (doesn't compose). Column selection stores **field names** (`Set<string>`) — survives pinning/reordering/virtualization/visibility. `clearSelectionSilent()` preserves `selectedColumns`. Row↔column mutual exclusion auto-handled inside `#buildEvent()`. Header chord: plain & plain-Shift+Click reserved for sort; **Ctrl/⌘+Click** toggles column, **Ctrl/⌘+Shift+Click** extends from anchor. Keyboard: **Ctrl/⌘+Space** toggles focused cell's column; **Ctrl/⌘+Shift+←/→** extends. Utility columns skip every column-axis path. Spec: `column-selection.ts`, `column-selection.spec.ts`.
- DECIDED (May 2026, #308 review): `selection-change.detail.selectedColumns` + `getSelectedColumns()` emit in **visible-column order**, not Set-insertion (would leak interaction history). `buildSelectionEvent` filters `selectableColumnFields(visibleColumns)`. `selectColumns` query batches via private `#setColumnSelection(fields)`: filter unknowns, set once, emit once, render once. `#applySelectionClasses()` MUST reset stale `aria-selected` on cells previously carrying `.selected`/`.column-selected`, **scoped** — clearing all wipes active-cell focused marker (e2e: `accessibility.spec.ts > keyboard navigation updates aria-selected`).
- DECIDED (May 2026, #284): clears selection when host swaps source `rows` to different size; gated on `data-change` with changed `sourceRowCount` (stored row indices resolve against post-processRows `_rows`). Closure-cached `lastSourceRowCount = -1`. In-place edits (same count) preserve. RULED OUT: clear on every `data-change` (wipes on cell edit); deep-equality (too expensive). Also auto-selects row entering edit in `mode: 'row'` so `getSelectedRows()` reflects what user edits. Listens `edit-open` (now `broadcast()`). Gating: skip if `mode !== 'row'`, if `isRowSelectable` false, if already selected. `multiSelect:false` replaces; else adds. NO `edit-close` listener (persists post-edit). Tests: `selection-editing-integration.spec.ts`.
- DECIDED (Jul 2026): range-mode right-click (`onCellMouseDown` `button === 2`) INSIDE an existing range preserves the selection (early `return`, no drag start) so the context menu acts on the whole range — no Ctrl needed. Right-click OUTSIDE any range falls through to normal behavior (clear + select clicked cell). Uses `isCellInAnyRange`. Only the secondary button is special-cased; left-click drag/clear is unchanged. File: [SelectionPlugin.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/selection/SelectionPlugin.ts) `onCellMouseDown`.

### Editing & Undo

**Editing** — OWNS: active cell, editor snapshots, changed rows, dirty tracking. HOOKS: processColumns, processRows, afterCellRender, afterRowRender, onCellClick, onKeyDown. EVENTS: cell-commit, row-commit, edit-open/close. TENSION: must handle row re-sorting during edit (caches sort result)

- RULE: editor detection in `EditingPlugin` MUST use `closest(FOCUSABLE_EDITOR_SELECTOR)`, never `matches()`. Non-focusable editor descendants (`<option>` inside `<select>`, spans inside `contenteditable`) fail `matches()`.
- DECIDED (May 2026, #347, popup-`<select>` keyboard): `mode: 'grid'` ArrowUp/Down does not navigate cells when keydown target is inside editor descendant; Enter on popup-`<option>` must bail without `preventDefault`/`stopPropagation` (in BOTH `editor-injection.ts` host-keydown AND `EditingPlugin.onKeyDown`). WHY: Chromium `<select>` popup walks focus SELECT→OPTION→OPTION (flips `#gridModeInputFocused`); `preventDefault` blocks native popup commit. Fix: `focusout` keeps flag when `related.closest(FOCUSABLE_EDITOR_SELECTOR)` non-null; ArrowUp/Down branch uses `event.target.closest(...)` as fallback. Enter on focused editor (no popup) still commits + blurs + focuses grid (sets `#gridModeEditLocked=true`). RULE: editor-chain keydown handlers MUST NOT `stopPropagation` in grid mode. Tests: `editing-integration.spec.ts > ArrowDown originating from a focused <select> editor` / `> Enter on an <option>`.
- DECIDED (Apr 2026): row-mode `ArrowUp`/`Down` in `EditingPlugin.onKeyDown` MUST NOT commit + jump to adjacent row while a row is in edit mode. Returns `true` (handled, no-op) so focused editor consumes the key natively — number spinners, `<select>` traversal, `<textarea>` caret, MUI autocomplete/datepicker/combobox all rely on ArrowUp/Down. Users must Enter/Esc/Tab/click out before arrows resume cell nav. CONSISTENT WITH: `mode: 'grid'` when input focused, `editing && colType === 'select'` early return in `core/internal/keyboard.ts`, Excel/Sheets. Tests: `editing-integration.spec.ts > arrow keys while a row is in edit mode (row mode)`.
- DECIDED (#250 — portal-overlay editors): `keydown` handler in `editor-injection.ts` MUST short-circuit on `e.defaultPrevented` BEFORE inspecting `e.key`. WHY: portal pickers (EDS/Downshift, MUI date) call `e.preventDefault()` on option-confirming Enter; without guard, plugin treats Enter as "exit row edit" and tears editor down before picker's commit lands (surfaces as #250 row-recycle `removeChild`). Tests: `editor-injection.spec.ts`. Click-to-select on portal overlays still needs `registerExternalFocusContainer` (#251).
- DECIDED (#251 — overlay-editor parity): two mechanisms make portal editors work without per-library glue. (1) Generic `aria-expanded="true" + aria-controls=<id>` fallback: `editor-injection.ts` (host keydown) AND `EditingPlugin.onKeyDown` (Enter while row-editing) AND `EditingPlugin` document-pointerdown (outside-click) call `isInsideOpenAriaOverlay(target, scopeEl)` — walks `[aria-expanded="true"][aria-controls]` triggers in active row, resolves panel via `getElementById`, treats target as inside editor when panel contains it. (2) Explicit React opt-in: `ColumnEditorContext.grid` populated in `editor-injection.ts` from `deps.grid`. Editors call `ctx.grid.registerExternalFocusContainer(panel)` OR use `useGridOverlay(panelRef, {open})`. Tests: `editing-overlay-aria.spec.ts`, `use-grid-overlay.spec.tsx`.

- DECIDED (Jul 2026, commitCellValue): EditingPlugin answers the core `commitCellValue` query (`#handleCommitCellValue`) so `grid.updateRow/updateRows` route programmatic mutations through the full edit pipeline (validation/dirty/history/cascade). `source:'history'` fast-path applies value + recomputes dirty but does NOT re-record history (undo loop fix). Editing hit 50.80 kB raw with this surface → plugin bundle budget bumped 50→55 kB ([vite.config.ts](libs/grid/vite.config.ts); editing is the outlier, most plugins well under). Full contract: grid-plugins.md inter-plugin-communication. Tests: `editing-integration.spec.ts > programmatic mutation routing via updateRow`.

**UndoRedo** — OWNS: undo/redo stacks. HOOKS: onKeyDown (Ctrl+Z/Y). DEPENDS: editing (required). Applies reverts via `grid.updateRow(id,{field:val},'history')` — the `'history'` source makes EditingPlugin apply-without-re-recording, breaking the undo→commit→record loop (`#suppressRecording` also guards the buffered path).

- DECIDED (Jul 2026, nestable transactions): `beginTransaction()`/`endTransaction()` are re-entrant via `#transactionDepth`. `beginTransaction` while one is open increments depth (does NOT throw); only the outermost `endTransaction` (depth→0) finalizes the buffer. WHY (TBW111 bug): paste routes through `updateRows` → fires one `cell-commit` PER cell synchronously; consumers that bracket each commit with `beginTransaction()` + `queueMicrotask(endTransaction)` (e.g. cargo-tracker `onCellCommit` grouping cascades) hit a nested `beginTransaction` before the microtask ran → old code threw `TRANSACTION_IN_PROGRESS`, which propagated out of the `updateRows` loop so only the FIRST cell committed (surfaced as "paste only fills one cell"). Nesting also coalesces the whole paste into ONE compound undo entry. `endTransaction` still throws `NO_TRANSACTION` at depth 0 (unbalanced end). Reset depth in `detach()` + `clearHistory()`. Removed the `TRANSACTION_IN_PROGRESS` diagnostic use. Tests: `UndoRedoPlugin.spec.ts > should nest re-entrant transactions`; `clipboard-fill-pipeline.spec.ts > does not throw when a cell-commit handler brackets each commit`.

### Sorting & Filtering

**MultiSort** — OWNS: sortModel[], cached sort result. HOOKS: processRows, onHeaderClick. QUERIES: sort:get-model, sort:set-model. EVENTS: sort-change. TENSION: caches sort during row edit to prevent edited row from jumping. INVARIANT: MultiSort is the authoritative sort source — Tree and GroupingRows must query `sort:get-model` when MultiSort is loaded, not maintain independent sort state (causes desync of sort indicators vs actual order)

**Filtering** — OWNS: filterModels Map, cached unique values. HOOKS: processRows, afterRender, onHeaderClick, afterCellRender. EVENTS: filter-change.

- INVARIANT: numeric ops (`greaterThan`/`>=`/`<`/`<=`/`between`) MUST exclude blank values (null/undefined/''/NaN) before coercion — JS implicit conversion leaks them (`null >= 0` true, `Number('') === 0`). Blanks match ONLY explicit `blank` operator. NaN treated as blank.
- DECIDED (Apr 2026): number filter panel's Apply clears filter when both bounds are still data-derived defaults — else `between(dataMin,dataMax)` silently excludes blank rows.
- DECIDED (Apr 2026): `getDataRows()` resolves unique-value source — prefers `sourceRows`, falls back to `grid.rows.filter(r => r.__loading !== true)` so ServerSide hosts get unique values from loaded blocks (excluding placeholders) without `valuesHandler`. Async `valuesHandler` remains canonical for full server-side dataset.

### Row Details

**MasterDetail** — OWNS: expanded rows, detail height, animation state. HOOKS: processColumns (expander), onCellClick, afterRowRender, getRowHeight, adjustVirtualStart. EVENTS: master-detail-toggle

- SHARED expander util `core/plugin/expander-column.ts` surface: `EXPANDER_COLUMN_FIELD`, `EXPANDER_COLUMN_WIDTH`, `isExpanderColumn`, `isUtilityColumn`, `findExpanderColumn`, `createExpanderColumnConfig`, `ExpanderColumnRenderer`. DECIDED (Jun 2026): `createExpanderContainer` + `EXPANDER_COLUMN_STYLES` removed — zero consumers (plugins build their own DOM; real styling is in the modular CSS layers). Do not reintroduce.

### Reordering

**ReorderColumns** — OWNS: column order, drag state, `BaseColumnConfig.lockPosition` augmentation. HOOKS: onCellMouseDown/Move/Up, afterRender. QUERIES: owns `canMoveColumn` (local check + aggregates `grid.query('canMoveColumn', column)` for vetoes from PinnedColumnsPlugin etc.).

- DECIDED: per-column lock = top-level `ColumnConfig.lockPosition` (sibling of `lockVisible`). Augmentation in `reorder-columns/types.ts`, NOT core. Legacy `meta.lockPosition`/`meta.suppressMovable` honored in `column-drag.ts#canMoveColumn` (top-level first).
- DECIDED: ReorderPlugin owns authoritative `canMoveColumn` query — other plugins (VisibilityPlugin panel-drag) MUST `grid.query<boolean>('canMoveColumn', column)`, treat any `false` as veto. Without ReorderPlugin: query returns `[]` (treat as non-reorderable). Plugin keeps eager local check so test mocks (`query: () => []`) work.
- DECIDED: all column-level flags top-level on `ColumnConfig` via module augmentation from owning plugin's `types.ts` — never `meta.<flag>`, never directly in core. Flags: `lockVisible` (core), `lockPosition` (reorder-columns), `lockPinning`/`pinned` (pinned-columns), `utility` (public, core), `checkboxColumn` (`@internal`, selection), `group` (grouping-columns).
- INVARIANT: plugin-owned flags MUST NOT appear on `grid.getAllColumns()` projection — only grid-universal fields (`field`, `header`, `visible`, `lockVisible`, `utility`). Plugins read raw config via `this.grid.columns.find(c => c.field === ...)`. Keeps core bytes proportional to grid-universal concerns.
- INVARIANT: new query type costs ZERO core bytes (string-routed via `manifest.queries`). Core growth = JSDoc on public type OR field added to a projection.
- DECIDED (Apr 2026): `ColumnConfig.utility` is **public** (was `@internal`). Umbrella "system column" flag (selection checkbox, expander, drag handle, action menu). Honored by Visibility (chooser filter), Reorder (`canMoveColumn` false), Print (hidden unless `printHidden: false`), Clipboard/Export (`resolveColumns()` skips), Selection (clicks ignored), Filtering (no filter UI). Convention: prefix `__`.

**ReorderRows** — OWNS: row order, drag state. HOOKS: onCellMouseDown/Move/Up. QUERIES: canMoveRow

**RowDragDrop** (#225) — OWNS: row order + cross-grid drag/drop session. ALIASES: `reorderRows`, `rowReorder` (legacy). HOOKS: processColumns (drag-handle col), onKeyDown (Ctrl+arrow), onCellClick, delegated dragstart/over/leave/drop/dragend. QUERIES: canMoveRow. EVENTS: row-move (back-compat), row-drag-start (cancelable), row-drag-end, row-drop (cancelable), row-transfer (both source + target). USES: `core/internal/drag-drop-registry.ts` (WeakRef session map shared across split bundles) + `plugins/shared/drag-drop-protocol.ts` (MIME constants, payload codec, drop-position math, auto-scroller, current-session tracker for sync canDrop).

- DECIDED (#225, alias rules): `reorder-rows/index.ts` MUST NOT re-export `ROW_DRAG_HANDLE_FIELD`/`RowMoveDetail` (collides in `all.ts`). Only `RowReorderPlugin` + `RowReorderConfig` re-exported. UMD/ESM bundle inlines full `RowDragDropPlugin` (~16 kB / 5.4 kB gz) — Vite `externalizeCore` only externalizes `../../components/`/`../../../`. Both under 50 kB plugin budget. Alias dedup in `PluginManager#collapseAliasDuplicates` keyed by **constructor identity**, not plugin name. Configs merge via `BaseGridPlugin.mergeConfigsFrom`: silent on equal scalars/refs, warns TBW023 once on dedupe, throws TBW025 on conflict. Dedup warning silenced under `import.meta.env.PROD`.
- DECIDED (#225, session lookup): Same-window cross-grid uses `currentSession` (module-level singleton in `shared/drag-drop-protocol.ts`) for sync `canDrop` during `dragover` (where `dataTransfer.getData()` returns `''`). Cross-window falls back to JSON via `dataTransfer.getData(TBW_ROW_DRAG_MIME)` + `deserializeRow`. WeakRef registry matters only for live-object recovery on **drop**.
- DECIDED (Apr 2026, cross-window via `BroadcastChannel`): target window can only mutate its own `document`; `document.getElementById(payload.sourceGridId)` returns null cross-window — legacy code silently no-op'd source-side removal + `row-transfer` + `dragAccepted` flip. Fix: module-level `BroadcastChannel('tbw-row-drag-drop')` shared by all plugin instances. After successful cross-grid drop where `findPeerOnGrid(payload.sourceGridId) === null`, target broadcasts `tbw-row-drag-drop:transfer` `{sourceGridId,toGridId,dropZone,rowIndices,toIndex,operation,serializedRows}`. Instances filter by matching `sourceGridId` + `dropZone`, run source-side path. Channel lazy + ref-counted + closed on last detach. INVARIANT: origin-scoped — cross-origin cannot coordinate; when undefined (old browsers, sandboxed iframes), target receives rows but source untouched — `row-transfer` is authoritative success signal. Same-window peer path preserved via sync `findPeerOnGrid`. Tests: `row-drag-drop.spec.ts > cross-window transfer (BroadcastChannel)`.
- DECIDED (Apr 2026, `dragFrom` + row-clone drag image): `dragFrom?: 'handle'|'row'|'both'` (default `'handle'`). `'row'` hides handle col unless `showDragHandle` explicit. `applyRowDraggable()` called from BOTH `afterRender` AND `onScrollRender` (recycling loses `draggable="true"`).
- INVARIANT: interactive descendants (`button,input,select,textarea,a[href],[contenteditable]`) never start drag — `INTERACTIVE_DRAG_SELECTORS` in `isInteractiveDragOrigin()`.
- INVARIANT (CSS-scoping): row-clone for `setDragImage` MUST be appended INSIDE `this.gridElement`, NOT `document.body` (all `core/styles/*.css` scoped under `tbw-grid{…}` + `--tbw-column-template` on host — body clone collapses). Clone `position:fixed; top/left:-10000px`, removed in `setTimeout(0)`. Do NOT add `opacity`/`box-shadow` (browser already ~70% translucency; multiplies; box-shadow blur captures as horizontal fade). Tests: `row-drag-drop.spec.ts`.

### Display

**Responsive** — OWNS: breakpoint-based column visibility. HOOKS: processColumns, getRowHeight.

- INVARIANT: column header _row_ ALWAYS hidden in card mode (unconditional CSS `tbw-grid[data-responsive] .header { display: none }`). `hideHeader` config does NOT control that — it gates per-card _field labels_ (`Name:` `::before` prefix).
- DECIDED (May 2026): `hideHeader` default = `false` (labels visible). Plugin sets `data-responsive-hide-header` on host in `#applyResponsiveState()` only when `isResponsive && hideHeader === true`; CSS `tbw-grid[data-responsive][data-responsive-hide-header] .data-grid-row:not(.group-row) > .cell::before { display: none }`. Attribute cleared leaving card mode. Files: `ResponsivePlugin.ts`, `responsive.css`.

**Tooltip** — OWNS: active tooltip, positioning. HOOKS: afterCellRender

**StickyRows** (#279) — OWNS: clone container `.tbw-sticky-rows` (overlay), clone cache by row index, displayed indices, push displacement. HOOKS: afterRender, onScrollRender, onScroll. READS: `grid.rows`, `grid._virtualization.{container,positionCache,rowHeight}`. CONFIG: `isSticky` (string field or `(row,index)=>unknown`), `mode: 'push'|'stack'` (default push), `maxStacked`, `className`.

- INVARIANT: clones are decorative — `aria-hidden="true"`, no `tabindex`, focus classes stripped. Live row stays in pool for keyboard + AT.
- INVARIANT: container is `position: absolute; top: 0` overlay INSIDE `.rows-viewport`, NOT a flex child of `.rows-body`. Flex child would push viewport down by sticky height; rows are translated in scroll coords that don't account for the shift → visible duplicate during push transition. Viewport `overflow: clip` hides live row underneath.
- INVARIANT: `getCurrentScrollTop()` MUST read `_virtualization.container.scrollTop` (live faux-scroll), NOT derive from `start * rowHeight` (lags up to `rowHeight - 1` px, causes "invisible until row#3" symptom).
- INVARIANT: clone cache MUST be primed while row is live (`findRenderedRow` returns null once scrolled out).
- INVARIANT: sticky qualifies when `offsetOf(idx) < scrollTop` (strict — equality = live row at top, cloning would duplicate). In `'stack'` mode: `offsetOf(idx) < scrollTop + cumulativeHeightOfStuck` (each latches at stack bottom, not viewport top). At `maxStacked`: stricter edge `offsetOf(idx) < scrollTop + sumStuck - heightOf(oldest)` so eviction happens after full cross; meanwhile container is translated `-distance` (cap `heightOf(oldest)`) to animate. Push-mode equivalent: `pushOffset = heightOfStuck - distance`. Do NOT add incoming clone during anticipation (duplicates live row + covers prior section).
- INVARIANT: `refreshDisplay` tracks `displayedIndices` as actually-appended set (missing-clone retries next refresh).
- INVARIANT: clones are `cloneNode` SNAPSHOTS. With async framework adapters (React/Vue `PortalManager` batches cell content via `queueMicrotask`+`flushSync`), a row recycled back into the window on scroll-up has its cell `data-row` set synchronously but content flushed a microtask LATER. A clone captured during the sync `onScrollRender` hook then holds the PREVIOUS row's content and is cached forever. FIX: `onScrollRender` calls `maybeScheduleSettlePass()` → `requestAfterRender()` (STYLE phase, rAF — runs AFTER the microtask flush) which re-runs `afterRender`. Only schedules when a sticky row is in the rendered window; coalesced via `settlePassScheduled` (cleared at `afterRender` start, so the settle pass itself doesn't loop). Symptom it fixes: scroll down → up → down pins a DIFFERENT row than the first time (only repros with async-rendered cells, not plain-text docs demos).
- DECIDED (Jun 2026, follow-up to settle pass): re-capturing a DISPLAYED clone from live DOM (`refreshClonesInWindow`) MUST happen ONLY in `afterRender` (the settle pass, post-microtask), NEVER synchronously in `onScrollRender`. WHY: two coupled bugs. (1) Permanent — `primeCloneCache` refreshes the cache but `refreshDisplay` only swaps container children when the displayed _set_ changes (`sameSet` short-circuit), so a stale displayed node survived a same-set scroll-up until the user hit the absolute top (set `[0]→[]→[0]` forced a rebuild). `afterRender`→`refreshClonesInWindow` replaces the displayed node directly. (2) One-frame FLASH — `onScrollRender` re-capturing displayed clones synchronously paints the recycled live row's not-yet-flushed (previous-row) content for one frame before the settle pass corrects it. Deferring ALL displayed-clone re-capture to `afterRender` (runs after the microtask flush) removes the stale paint with no lost update: a settle pass is always scheduled whenever a sticky row is in the window — exactly when `refreshClonesInWindow` has work. `onScrollRender` keeps `primeCloneCache` (cache-only, invisible) + `refreshDisplay` + `maybeScheduleSettlePass`. Tests: `sticky-rows.spec.ts > never paints stale content on the displayed clone during a scroll-up recycle (no flash)`.
- TENSION: fast scroll-jump past sticky's render window before `onScrollRender` fires → clone unprimed until user scrolls back. Fix would require synthesising from data (re-couples to `core/internal/rows.ts`).
- DECIDED (Nov 2025, #279): zero core bytes. WHY: plugin reads `_virtualization` internals (same pattern as `MasterDetailPlugin.ts:577`). RULED OUT: `core/internal/rows.ts.renderInlineRow` (#240 — module-level `document.createElement('template')` crashes happy-domless tests). Files: `StickyRowsPlugin.ts`, `sticky-rows.css`. Bundle: 4.01 kB gz ESM. Tests: `sticky-rows.spec.ts` (16).

**ContextMenu** — OWNS: menu items, open state. HOOKS: afterRender, onKeyDown. QUERIES: getContextMenuItems (queries all plugins for contributions)

### Export

**Clipboard** — OWNS: clipboard buffer. HOOKS: onKeyDown (Ctrl+C/V/X). DEPENDS: selection (optional).

- DECIDED (Jul 2026, structured same-grid + cross-window paste payload): copy writes WYSIWYG `text/plain` (via `processCell`) for external targets AND (a) snapshots RAW cell values in-memory (`#internalClipboard = { text, rawRows }`, cloned, same col order as text) AND (b) writes `text/html` = a real `<table>` of display text whose root carries a base64 `data-tbw-clip` structured payload (`copyToClipboard(text, html)` uses `ClipboardItem` write, falls back to `writeText`). On paste, values source precedence: (1) in-memory match (`text === #internalClipboard.text`, exact — preserves types like `Date`, no serialize) → (2) `parseClipboardHtmlPayload(getData('text/html'))` for **cross-grid / cross-window / post-reload** → (3) parsed text. Whichever wins becomes `PasteDetail.rawRows`; `defaultPasteHandler` uses `rawRows ?? rows` and clones each value (`cloneStructured`) so tiled/multi-target pastes never share a ref. Array→cells (equal/unequal, expansion, clip, fill-selection tiling) all work — `rawRows` replaces `pastedRows` uniformly. Payload codec lives in its OWN module [clipboard-payload.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/clipboard/clipboard-payload.ts) — MUST NOT put it in copy.ts/paste.ts: paste.ts importing a VALUE from copy.ts created a **circular import** (TDZ `X is not defined` at test load). Cross-app HTML injection is gated by the editing/validation commit pipeline. Tests: `clipboard.spec.ts > carries raw structured values`, `> recovers raw values from a cross-window paste via the text/html payload`, `> round-trips a structured payload through buildClipboardHtml → parseClipboardHtmlPayload`, `> clones structured values per target`.

- DECIDED (Jul 2026, fillSelection tiling): `ClipboardConfig.fillSelection` (default `false`) tiles a smaller clipboard source across a larger **bounded** selection via modulo indexing (1 cell → fill all; `v1,v2` → `v1,v2,v1,v2`; 2×2 → 4×4). Policy in plugin, mechanism in handler: `#handleNativePaste` sets `PasteDetail.fillSelection` (only when `bounds != null`) and widens `fields` to full selection width (`maxCol-targetCol+1`); `defaultPasteHandler` ([types.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/clipboard/types.ts)) branches on `fillSelection && target.bounds`, iterating selection bounds with `pastedRows[r%srcRows]` / `sourceRow[c%srcCols]`. Never grows grid; non-editable columns still skipped. `detail.rows` stays the literal source (not pre-tiled) so listeners see what was copied. RULED OUT: pre-tiling `parsed` in the plugin (mutates detail, wastes memory on big selections).
- DECIDED (Jul 2026, paste target = bounding box of ALL ranges): `#handleNativePaste` MUST compute `targetRow/targetCol` + `bounds` from the min/max over **every** `selection.ranges` entry, NOT `ranges[0]`. WHY (live bug): a real range-mode selection of N cells in a column is stored as N **single-cell** ranges (cell-by-cell click/drag, often with duplicates) — e.g. `[{3,6→3,6},{4,6→4,6},{4,6→4,6},{5,6→5,6}]`. Reading `ranges[0]` alone saw a 1×1 range → `isMultiCell=false` → `bounds=null` → `fillSelection=false` → only the first cell pasted (the reported "fillSelection doesn't work"). Bounding box also normalizes reversed (bottom-up / right-to-left) drags. `isMultiCell = mode∈{range,row} && (selMinRow!==selMaxRow || selMinCol!==selMaxCol)`. Diagnosed via Chrome DevTools MCP capturing the live `selection.getSelection()` at paste time — the programmatic `setRanges([oneContiguousRange])` path masked it (worked), so the repro test uses **multiple single-cell ranges**. Tests: `clipboard-fill-pipeline.spec.ts > fills when the selection is several single-cell ranges`.
- DECIDED (Jul 2026, paste target anchor fallback): `#handleNativePaste` resolves the target cell as `firstRange ? boundingBoxTopLeft : (selection.anchor ?? {0,0})`. WHY (live bug): **range mode CLEARS `selection.ranges` on plain keyboard navigation** (arrow/Tab without Shift — see `SelectionPlugin` afterRender `pendingKeyboardUpdate` branch), keeping the focused cell only as the `anchor`. So "copy a cell, arrow to another cell, paste" left `ranges` empty → the old `: 0` fallback pasted into cell **(0,0)** (col 0 = first column) regardless of the active cell; downstream, consumer month-validation then made non-date values no-op and date values land in (0,0), which read as "only date columns paste, always into row 0". Mirrors the focused-cell fallback already in `#handleCopy`. `anchor` is a **visible-column** index (matches `_visibleColumns[targetCol]`). Tests: `clipboard.spec.ts > should target the selection anchor when ranges are empty`.
- DECIDED (Jul 2026, COPY target anchor fallback + stale-attr fix): `#handleCopy` with empty `ranges` now resolves the active cell via `selection.anchor` FIRST (visible-col index → `visibleColumns[anchor.col].field`), then the focused DOM cell. WHY (live bug, Chrome DevTools MCP verified): after keyboard nav `ranges=[]` AND `document.activeElement` is NOT a cell (`activeIsCell:false`), so the DOM-only fallback found nothing → **keyboard-nav Ctrl+C silently no-op'd** (mouse copy worked because click makes a range). Also `#getFocusedCellFromDOM` queried `[data-field-cache]`/`dataset.fieldCache` — an attribute that NEVER existed anywhere (cells carry `data-field`/`data-row`/`data-col`, see `constants.ts` `ATTR.FIELD='data-field'` and `#getCellText`); fixed to `.cell[data-field]` + `dataset.field`. `anchor` (visible index) and the DOM-fallback (`this.columns` full index via `findIndex`) live in different index spaces — resolve each to a `field` separately, never mix. Tests: `clipboard.spec.ts > should copy the anchor cell when ranges are empty`, `> should copy the focused DOM cell when there is no anchor`.
- DECIDED (Jul 2026, header strip on paste): `#dropCopiedHeaderRow` in [ClipboardPlugin.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/clipboard/ClipboardPlugin.ts) removes a copied header row from `parsed` BEFORE building `PasteDetail`, so a round-trip paste (with `includeHeaders: true`) never lands a header label in a cell — even though copied text still carries the header for spreadsheet/notepad round-trips. Guarded: strips only when (1) `config.includeHeaders` AND (2) `parsed.length > 1` AND (3) **every cell of `parsed[0]` is one of this grid's column labels** (`#columnLabels()` = Set of `header || field` over ALL columns). Match is against the full label SET, NOT the target column — so a **cross-column** paste (copy "Source terminal" → paste into "Terminal route") strips the header too; the earlier target-column-only match left the source header in the cell and pushed values down a row (Jul 2026 bug: cargo-tracker capture2). External pastes (first row ≠ any labels) left intact. Label set uses `col.header || col.field` to match `buildClipboardText`. Tests: `clipboard.spec.ts > should strip a copied header from a different column (cross-column paste)`.
- INVARIANT (clipboard paste editability): paste writes ONLY to cells the EditingPlugin approves via the `getCellEditableResolver` query — a `(field,row)=>boolean` predicate applying `#isCellEditable` (rowEditable gate + `editable` true/false/**function**; row-conditional IS honored). No EditingPlugin loaded → query unanswered → predicate defaults to `() => false` → paste is a no-op (and `editable` on a column throws at config validation without the plugin). Editability is resolved against the PRE-paste row snapshot. NO type/value validation on paste: raw string is written straight to `grid.rows` (pasting `"abc"` into a number column stores `"abc"`). Coercion/validation is the implementor's job — via custom `pasteHandler`, the `paste` event, or column `format`. Interactive editors coerce; paste bypasses editors.
- DECIDED (Jul 2026, paste routes through updateRows): `defaultPasteHandler` ([types.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/clipboard/types.ts)) routes edits through `grid.updateRows(updates,'paste')` for rows with a resolvable `getRowId` — so paste participates in editing's dirty tracking, history (undoable), validation, and abortion (via the `commitCellValue` query; see grid-plugins.md). Rows without an id (or where `getRowId` throws), plus freshly-grown empty rows for unbounded pastes, fall back to direct `Object.assign` (grid grown first). Editability still filtered up-front via `getCellEditableResolver`. This SUPERSEDES the "raw string written straight to grid.rows, no validation" note above FOR the id-backed path — a `cell-commit` handler can now coerce/veto pasted values. Paste mutates in place (like an edit). Tests: `clipboard.spec.ts` covers the direct fallback (mock has no `getRowId`); pipeline routing is covered by editing/undo specs.
- DECIDED (Jul 2026, per-column onPaste + paste source): two-layer per-cell paste control. (1) Column `onPaste?: boolean | (ctx: PasteCellContext) => boolean | { value }` (clipboard-augmented onto `BaseColumnConfig`) is applied in `defaultPasteHandler`'s `addEdit` chokepoint AFTER the `getCellEditableResolver` check: `false` (or `()=>false`) skips the cell (alignment preserved, like non-editable); `{ value }` rewrites it. Return `{ value }` (not a bare value) disambiguates transform from a boolean verdict so boolean columns stay safe. Sync only. (2) Paste commits with edit-origin `'paste'` (was `'api'`), so a `cell-commit` listener tells a paste from a user edit via `detail.source === 'paste'`. `onPaste` is the ergonomic per-column path (reject/transform); `source` is the general path for global commit validators. Types: `PasteCellContext`, augmentation in [types.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/clipboard/types.ts). `PasteCellContext` also carries `sourceField` (the origin column the value was copied FROM) so `onPaste` can reject cross-column/cross-type pastes ("no car into a fruit column"). Source fields are already in the copy payload (`ClipboardPayload.fields`); threaded via `#internalClipboard.fields` (in-memory same-grid) / `payload.fields` (cross-grid HTML) → `PasteDetail.sourceFields` → per-cell `sourceFields?.[colOffset]` (mod `srcCols` when tiling). `undefined` for external (Excel) pastes. Full source column DEFINITION is NOT offered (not serializable cross-grid); field name is the identifier.
- DECIDED (Jul 2026, paste-rejected event + custom-handler helpers): `onPaste` rejections are surfaced via a `paste-rejected` event (`PasteRejectedDetail = { rejected: PasteRejectedCell[] }`, reason `'column'`=`onPaste:false` | `'cell'`=callback returned false), emitted ONCE per paste from `defaultPasteHandler` (even when every cell rejected → fires before the all-rejected early-return). Non-editable skips are NOT reported (silent, by design). To fix the "custom `pasteHandler` bypasses `onPaste`" gap, the resolution logic is extracted into exported pure helper `resolveColumnPaste(onPaste, ctx) → { accepted:true, value } | { accepted:false, reason }` + `emitPasteRejected(grid, rejected[])`; `defaultPasteHandler` uses both, and `ClipboardConfig.pasteHandler` JSDoc documents that custom handlers MUST call them to honor `onPaste`/emit the event. Emit uses `grid.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('paste-rejected',{bubbles:true}))` (matches base-plugin `emit`). Exported from clipboard `index.ts` (→ `/all` via `export *`). Adapter parity: `paste-rejected` wired in all 3 clipboard directives + added to Angular `_intentionallyOmittedEvents` (else `_assertEventOutputMapCoversCore` fails the build; React `satisfies` is prop→event only so an unwired event does NOT break React/Vue). Files: [types.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/clipboard/types.ts).
- DECIDED (Nov 2025, paste perf): `parseClipboardText` ([paste.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/clipboard/paste.ts)) MUST extract cells via single `normalized.slice(cellStart, i)`, not char-by-char `currentCell += char`. State machine still scans char-by-char for `inQuotes`/delimiter/newline tracking. WHY: bench [clipboard.bench.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/clipboard/clipboard.bench.ts) — 10K-row no-quotes 168→455 hz (+172%), mixed 164→289 hz (+77%). Quoted cells go through `unquoteCell()` (rare path).
- DECIDED (Nov 2025, format perf): `formatCellValue` (`copy.ts`) and `formatCsvValue` ([csv.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/export/csv.ts)) MUST dispatch `typeof === 'string'` first, before `instanceof Date`. Strings dominate; `instanceof` is non-trivial. WHY: `formatCsvValue` "string with comma" 5.66M→6.25M hz (+10%), `buildCsv` 10K rows 57.9→77.7 hz (+34% compounding). `processCell` escape hatch + config-driven quoting still checked first.

**Export** — OWNS: export format/state. Exposes download methods (`exportCsv`/`exportExcel`/`exportJson`) AND data accessors (`export()`/`formatCsv()`/`formatExcel()`/`getResolvedColumns()`) for ExcelJS/clipboard/server-upload hand-off. `mode: 'raw' | 'formatted'` (default raw; formatted applies type defaults + `column.format`).

- DECIDED (#240): plugins MUST NOT import from `core/internal/rows.ts` at module scope — module-level `document.createElement('template')` crashes happy-domless tests + pollutes plugin bundles. Inline small helpers (`resolveFormat`) instead.
- DECIDED (#240): pre-resolving rows for downstream `buildCsv`/`buildExcelXml` (which re-call `resolveCellValue`) MUST strip `column.valueAccessor` from passed columns — accessors mis-resolve against synthetic `Record<string,unknown>` keyed by `field`, produce `undefined`/`NaN`.

**Print** — OWNS: print styling. Exposes print methods

### Pinned Rows

**PinnedRows** — OWNS: pinned row positions (top/bottom), info bar (counts/panels), aggregation rows. HOOKS: afterRender. READS FROM: `grid.sourceRows` (`totalRows`), `grid.rows` (`filteredRows`), filter plugin `cachedResult` (preferred when present), selection plugin `selected`.

- INVARIANT: `filteredRows` reflects post-filter count regardless of mechanism (filter plugin / column filters / external/server-side / direct `grid.rows =`). DECIDED (Apr 2026): `buildContext` derives counts from live grid state, not the `rows` arg — externally-filtered hosts get correct counts without the filter plugin.
- DECIDED (Apr 2026, #255 unified slots): `slots[]` replaces parallel `aggregationRows[]` + `customPanels[]`. Each slot = one DOM row. Discriminator = **presence of `render`** → `PanelSlot`, else `AggregationSlot`. When `slots` set, ALL legacy fields ignored. Legacy without `slots` keeps byte-identical DOM via `synthesizeLegacySlots`. `PanelRender` returns `HTMLElement | null` — null drops contribution; panel with all-null renderers dropped entirely (how `selectedCountPanel()` self-hides on 0 selected). Top slots render inside NEW `.tbw-header-pinned` AFTER `.header`; bottom in `.tbw-footer`. Wrapper hidden under `tbw-grid[data-responsive]`. Adapters propagate null (React null/false/undefined; Vue null/undefined; Angular always rendered).
- INVARIANT: built-in `rowCountPanel`/`selectedCountPanel`/`filteredCountPanel` exported from `@toolbox-web/grid/plugins/pinned-rows`. Hardcoded English (`"Total: N rows"`) lives inside — i18n consumers build own renderer (do NOT add locale options).
- DECIDED (May 2026, demo-loop fix): `renderPanelSlot(slot, context, previousRow?)` is **ref-cached** — returns `previousRow` when every output is ref-equal; `populateSlotWrapper` diffs by ref, skips `replaceChildren`. WHY: framework consumers mounting components into returned container were torn down + remounted every `afterRender` (~30Hz), bouncing rows viewport through ResizeObserver autosizer = infinite loop. Built-in panels create fresh element per call so ref-equality fails and they keep updating. Consumer contract: return same element ref across calls to opt into stable rendering.

## shell-plugin (extraction #370 — v3 opt-in LANDED)

> Shell = header bar + toolbar content + tool panels. Lives entirely in a built-in `ShellPlugin` ([plugins/shell/](libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/shell/)). **v3: shell is OPT-IN** — a bare `<tbw-grid>` renders NO shell chrome. Activate via `features: { shell: <ShellConfig|true> }` OR `plugins: [new ShellPlugin(<ShellConfig>)]`. Core has ZERO shell value-references (tree-shaken out of `index.js`). HARD RULE: core MUST NOT reference any plugin (enforced by lint).

- DECIDED (#370 v3 — auto-register REMOVED): core no longer prepends `new ShellPlugin()`. The `#initializePlugins` auto-register block, the `#shellPlugin` field, `#resolveShellPlugin()`, the `import { ShellPlugin }` (old `SHELL-AUTOREGISTER-V3-370` marker), and all `TEMP-BUDGET-370`/`TEMP-SHELL-IMPORT` markers are GONE (0 grep matches). Shell self-inits its state in `ShellPlugin.ensureState(grid)` on attach; absent shell ⇒ no chrome. `index.js` 145kB raw / 42.46kB gz (budget 170/50/45 restored in vite.config.ts); shell chunk 40.72kB/10.99gz.
- DECIDED (#370 v3 — grid-element delegates REMOVED): the 15 `@deprecated` shell methods/getters on the grid element (`open`/`close`/`toggleToolPanel`, `toggleToolPanelSection`, `get`/`register`/`unregisterToolPanel`, `get`/`register`/`unregister`{HeaderContent,ToolbarContent}, `isToolPanelOpen`/`expandedToolPanelSections`) + `#warnShellApiDeprecated`/`#shellApiWarned` + `TBW076 SHELL_API_DEPRECATED` are all DELETED. Public API is now ONLY `grid.getPluginByName('shell').<method>` (typed via `PluginNameMap['shell']`). `refreshShellHeader()` is KEPT on the grid element (`@internal` bridge — shell-controller + Angular adapter call it). Test file deleted: `core/shell-deprecation.spec.ts`. Migration in specs: top-level `shell:` config → `features: { shell }`; register panels AFTER `await waitUpgrade(grid)` via `const shell = grid.getPluginByName('shell')` (the plugin instance does not exist pre-connect).
- INVARIANT (#370 v3 — empty opt-in shell chrome bootstrap): an empty `features: { shell: true }` (no title/panels/header content) renders NO chrome, so the controller stays `initialized=false`. The 4 controller register methods (`registerToolPanel`/`registerHeaderContent`/`registerToolbarContent` in [shell-controller.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/shell/shell-controller.ts)) MUST bootstrap chrome when connected-but-not-initialized: guard is `if (initialized || grid._hostElement.isConnected)` → `grid.refreshShellHeader?.()` (header-content keeps the lightweight `renderHeaderContent` path only when ALREADY initialized, else bootstraps). Use `grid._hostElement.isConnected`, NOT `grid.isConnected` (the `GridElement`/`ShellControllerGrid` type has no `isConnected` → TS2339 at build, though it works at runtime in vitest). WHY: v2's always-on shell always had chrome so the first API registration never hit this; v3's opt-in shell needs the first panel/header/toolbar registration to build the wrapper. `initialState`/`defaultOpen`/`locked` all re-apply on post-upgrade registration (the structural rebuild re-runs the restore logic). Tests: `grid.integration.spec.ts` shell describe block (107 pass).
- DECIDED (#374, resolve shell by NAME not class): `#resolveShellPlugin()` + the disconnect cleanup MUST resolve via `#pluginManager.getPluginByName('shell')`, NOT `getPlugin(ShellPlugin)`. WHY: `pluginMap` is keyed by `plugin.constructor` ([plugin-manager.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/core/plugin/plugin-manager.ts#L170)); in the DIST build core's `index.js` bundles its OWN ShellPlugin copy while `lib/plugins/shell/index.js` (where the `features/*`+`plugins/*` subpath aliases resolve) is a SEPARATE copy → two class identities. Constructor-identity lookup misses the feature-attached instance ⇒ core never calls `ensureState` on it ⇒ `hasState===false` ⇒ `processConfig`/`afterStructuralRender` early-return ⇒ NO shell chrome (e2e `.tbw-shell-title` absent in all 4 demos; passes in vitest/source where one class exists). Name lookup is immune. Regression test: `features/shell.spec.ts` "renders the shell when the attached plugin class differs from core" (a `class extends ShellPlugin` subclass = distinct ctor, same `name`, reproduces it). INVARIANT: any future core→plugin instance lookup that must survive dist chunk-duplication MUST use `getPluginByName`, never `getPlugin(Class)`.
- DECIDED (#370 v3 — TBW076 deprecation warning REMOVED): the `#warnShellApiDeprecated`/`#shellApiWarned` once-per-method guard and `SHELL_API_DEPRECATED='TBW076'` diagnostic are deleted (the delegates they guarded are gone). HISTORICAL LESSON (still applies to any future delegate deprecation): before warning on a public delegate that first-party code also calls, REPOINT internal callers to the plugin instance first, else the lib warns about itself.
- DECIDED (Phase 2, lint seam guard): [eslint.config.mjs](eslint.config.mjs) scopes `no-restricted-imports` to `libs/grid/src/lib/core/**/*.ts` (ignores `*.spec.ts`), forbidding VALUE imports matching `**/plugins/shell{,/**}` with `allowTypeImports: true`. `core/types.ts` `import type` of `*Impl`/`ShellState` is allowed. **v3: there is NO sanctioned core→shell value import** — the old grid.ts L4 exception is gone, so the rule now forbids ALL core→shell value imports outright. This formally enforces the HARD RULE.
- DECIDED (Phase 2, plugin→shell dependencies): plugins that need a shell host declare it via `static dependencies` (validated by `validatePluginDependencies`). `VisibilityPlugin`: `{name:'shell',required:true}` (+ `{name:'reorder',required:false}`) → **v3: THROWS TBW020 if shell absent** (in v2 the auto-register masked it; now a bare grid + VisibilityPlugin with no shell feature errors). `PivotPlugin`: `{name:'shell',required:false,severity:'warn',when:cfg=>cfg.showToolPanel===true}` → WARNS TBW021 only when its tool panel is on. `PrintPlugin`: no shell dependency. Tests: `validate-config.spec.ts` "shell dependencies (#370)"; `visibility-plugin.spec.ts`.
- DECIDED (light-DOM decoupling): the THREE shell `registerLightDomHandler('tbw-grid-header'|'tbw-grid-tool-buttons'|'tbw-grid-tool-panel', …)` + `handleShellChange` live in ShellPlugin `attach()`; `unregisterLightDomHandler` in `detach()`. Core keeps ONLY `tbw-grid-column`/`tbw-grid-detail` + generic `observeLightDOM(this)`. `MutationObserver` infra stays in `ConfigManager` ([config-manager.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/core/internal/config-manager.ts#L896)) — tag-agnostic, ignores tags with no handler. INVARIANT: shell plugin absent ⇒ no shell handler ⇒ `<tbw-grid-header>` yields zero callbacks ⇒ zero config/state change. Unclaimed light-DOM config elements stay VISIBLE (the `headerEl.style.display='none'` hide is a ShellPlugin side effect) — an intended "this config is doing nothing" signal for a missing import. MUST NOT add a core CSS rule hiding SHELL / plugin-owned tags (`tbw-grid-header`/`tbw-grid-tool-buttons`/`tbw-grid-tool-panel`/`tbw-grid-detail`) — that would reintroduce core→plugin coupling AND erase the missing-import signal. EXCEPTION (#tbw-grid-type, Jul 2026): CORE-owned config tags that core ALWAYS consumes — `tbw-grid-column` and `tbw-grid-type` — ARE hidden by a core CSS rule (`base.css`: `tbw-grid > tbw-grid-column, tbw-grid > tbw-grid-type { display:none }`). WHY: these carry inline `<tbw-grid-column-view>`/`<tbw-grid-column-editor>` `{{ }}` templates that would otherwise render their raw markup literally above the grid (there is no "forgot to import" scenario — core is always the consumer). Hiding does not affect parsing or adapter template registration (both read from `display:none` elements).
- DECIDED (id collision precedence): a tool-panel/header-content id declared by BOTH a plugin and light-DOM ⇒ **PLUGIN wins**; explicit API registration wins over plugin (API > plugin > light-DOM). `TBW073`/`TBW074` warn on each override. Order-independent — enforced on both sides: `#collectPluginShellContributions` (grid.ts) overrides+cleans+warns unless id ∈ `apiToolPanelIds`/`apiHeaderContentIds`; `parseLightDomToolPanels` (shell.ts) skips+hides+warns when id ∈ `toolPanels` but ∉ `lightDomToolPanelIds`. Test: `shell.spec.ts` "lets a plugin-owned panel win over a colliding light-DOM panel (#370)".
- DECIDED (type ownership): shell config types canonical in [plugins/shell/types.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/shell/types.ts) (`ShellConfig`, `ShellHeaderConfig`, `ToolPanelConfig`, `ToolbarContentDefinition`, `ToolPanelDefinition`, `HeaderContentDefinition`). **v3: the 6 `@deprecated` re-alias types are DELETED from `core/types.ts` and from `public.ts`'s `./lib/core/types` export block** — they now export ONLY from `@toolbox-web/grid/plugins/shell` (subpath via `plugins/*` wildcard). Core modules that need these types `import type { X } from '../plugins/shell/types'` (grid.ts, core/plugin/types.ts, base-plugin.ts, VisibilityPlugin, PivotPlugin, PrintPlugin). `GridConfig.shell` lives in core interface only via the plugin's `declare module '../../core/types' { interface GridConfig { shell?: ShellConfig } }` augmentation (NON-deprecated — the supported config path). `ShellState` stays in `plugins/shell/shell.ts`; core imports it `import type`. ADAPTERS MUST import `HeaderContentDefinition`/`ToolbarContentDefinition`/`ShellConfig` from `@toolbox-web/grid/plugins/shell`, never the package root.
- DECIDED (CSS ownership split — CORRECTED): BOTH `.tbw-scroll-area` AND `.tbw-grid-content` are CORE layout and live in `core/styles/base.css`. `dom-builder.ts` `buildGridDOM` builds a `.tbw-grid-content` wrapper for EVERY grid — the no-shell branch ([dom-builder.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/core/internal/dom-builder.ts#L151) `div('tbw-grid-content')`) wraps `.tbw-scroll-area` directly; with a shell, `rebuildShellDOM` WRAPS `.tbw-shell-body` around that SAME pre-existing `.tbw-grid-content` (it does NOT create it). INVARIANT: a CSS rule for any DOM the core dom-builder always renders MUST live in `base.css`, NEVER in a plugin stylesheet — plugin CSS only loads when that plugin is registered, and the shell became OPT-IN at v3 (#260/#261/#262). RULED OUT: putting `.tbw-grid-content { flex:1; display:flex; min-height:0 }` in `shell.css` — caused EVERY shell-less grid (all docs demos + default consumer grids) to collapse the `flex:1` scroll-area to a single row (37px) and clip all data rows; the unstyled wrapper defaulted to `display:block` + full virtual height. Fixed by moving the rule to `base.css` (parallels the #370 `.tbw-scroll-area` move). Shell `shell.css` owns only `.tbw-shell-*`, `.tbw-toolbar-*`, `.tbw-tool-panel*`, `.tbw-accordion*`.
- DECIDED (#375, `toolPanel.mode: 'dropdown'` — whole sidebar as anchored popover): third mode beside `'overlay'`/`'push'`. WHY: compact toolbars + bring-your-own trigger (e.g. a custom column-header "columns" button) want the full accordion floating in the top layer, not a docked sidebar. IMPL: dom-builder/`renderShellBody` add `popover="manual"` to `.tbw-tool-panel` ONLY in dropdown mode (NOT `auto` — `manual` stops the platform light-dismiss from racing a consumer toggle button). Show/hide via `showToolPanelDropdown`/`hideToolPanelDropdown` ([shell.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/shell/shell.ts)) called from `shell-controller.ts` `openToolPanel`/`closeToolPanel`. Positioning: CSS anchor positioning (`anchor-name`/`position-anchor` + `position-try-fallbacks`) behind `@supports (anchor-name: --x)`; JS `positionDropdownFallback` (fixed `top`/`left`/`right` from `getBoundingClientRect`) otherwise. Anchor priority: explicit `OpenToolPanelOptions.anchor` (new `@since 2.16.0` interface) → `[data-panel-toggle]` → grid corner (`grid._hostElement`, sets `data-anchor='corner'`). `state.dropdownAnchorEl` tracks the resolved anchor so `setupClickOutsideDismiss` ignores clicks on it (no toggle race). Dropdown ALWAYS light-dismisses (Escape via `setupEscapeDismiss`, click-outside) regardless of `closeOnClickOutside`. ALL Popover/anchor calls guarded (`typeof panel.showPopover === 'function'`, `CSS.supports(...)`) — happy-dom lacks both, so tests assert attribute/`dataset.anchor`/state, not top-layer render. Resize handle hidden, popover capped `max-height: min(70vh, 480px)` w/ internal scroll. INVARIANT: dropdown CSS MUST re-assert `.tbw-tool-panel[popover] { display: none }` + open via `&:popover-open, &.open { display: flex }` — the base `.tbw-tool-panel { display: flex }` author rule (same `@layer tbw-base`) outranks the UA `[popover]:not(:popover-open){display:none}` origin and would otherwise leak the CLOSED popover on top of the header (#375 follow-up). The `&.open` arm is the no-Popover-API fallback: `updatePanelState` JS-toggles `.open` regardless of mode, so when `showPopover()` is unavailable (older browsers / test DOM, `:popover-open` never matches) the dropdown still renders (#376 review). INVARIANT (multi-grid, #375 follow-up): two collaborating per-grid mechanisms MUST be grid-scoped or two dropdown grids on one page corrupt each other. (a) `showToolPanelDropdown` mints a UNIQUE `anchor-name` per show (`--tbw-tool-panel-anchor-${++seq}`, stored in `state.dropdownAnchorName`) — a shared constant name pairs grid A's popover with grid B's trigger (off-screen render). (b) `setupClickOutsideDismiss` matches `.tbw-tool-panel`/`[data-panel-toggle]` ONLY via `isInGrid(node, gridElement)` (`gridElement.contains` OR `node.getRootNode().host === gridElement`) — an unscoped match treats ANOTHER grid's toggle as "inside" and leaves grid A stuck open when grid B opens. Tests: `shell.spec.ts` (render popover attr, click-outside anchor-ignore, dropdown helpers), `grid.integration.spec.ts` (popover open/close + anchor resolution). Demo: `apps/docs/.../demos/shell/ShellDropdownDemo.astro` (custom column-header trigger). Deprecated `grid.ts` `openToolPanel`/`toggleToolPanel` wrappers NOT threaded with options (v3-removed path).
- DECIDED (#378, dropdown re-anchor via MutationObserver — pre-paint, in-place, no flash): a column-visibility toggle/reorder fires `afterRender` (STYLE phase), NOT `afterStructuralRender` — so wiring `reanchorOpenDropdown` only into `#renderShellContents` (structural path) MISSED the real-world bug. The trigger is often owned by an OUTER framework (e.g. a React `headerRenderer` `[data-panel-toggle]` button) that commits the replacement node AFTER the grid's synchronous render. MECHANISM (replaces the earlier deferred-rAF approach): while a dropdown panel is open, `ShellPlugin` ([ShellPlugin.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/shell/ShellPlugin.ts)) runs a `MutationObserver` (`#reanchorObserver`/`#reanchorObserving`, lazy-started in `afterRender`, scoped to `grid._renderRoot`, `{childList:true,subtree:true}` — NOT attributes, so the re-pair's own inline-style writes don't re-trigger it) that calls `reanchorOpenDropdown()` on every DOM mutation. WHY observer beats rAF: MutationObserver callbacks run as a PRE-PAINT microtask, so the re-pair lands before the browser paints the dangling-anchor frame → NO flash; it is event-driven so it never misses a late React commit → the dropdown no longer intermittently disappears. `afterRender` ALSO calls `reanchorOpenDropdown()` synchronously (backstop for grid-owned synchronous trigger recreation) and starts the observer; observer disconnected on panel close, grid disconnect (`disposeShellState`), and when state goes away. happy-dom/SSR (no `MutationObserver`) rely on the synchronous `afterRender` call. RE-PAIR IS IN-PLACE: `reanchorOpenDropdown` prefers `repairDropdownAnchor(renderRoot, state, trigger)` ([shell.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/shell/shell.ts)) which MOVES the already-minted `anchor-name` onto the fresh `[data-panel-toggle]` and clears stale `top`/`left`/`right` WITHOUT re-entering the Popover API (`showToolPanelDropdown` re-show mints a new name + flashes to corner for a frame). `repairDropdownAnchor` is gated by `supportsAnchorPositioning()` (now exported) in the controller; when unsupported it falls back to `showToolPanelDropdown(fresh,false)` fixed-coord re-show. `reanchorOpenDropdown` keeps the idempotent FAST-PATH (no-op when the stored trigger is connected AND still carries `state.dropdownAnchorName`). Verified live (Equinor Planning app): 10-click synchronous burst keeps panel `data-anchor='below'`, paired, visible, stable `{top:252,left:1288}` — no flash, no disappearance. Tests: `shell.spec.ts` "repairDropdownAnchor" (in-place move / no-name / no-panel) + reanchor suite.
- DECIDED (#379 review hardening): three robustness fixes on the #378 mechanism. (1) `#startReanchorObserver` now calls `#stopReanchorObserver()` before its early return when `shell.toolPanel.mode !== 'dropdown'` — flipping mode away from dropdown while the panel stays open used to leave the observer watching `_renderRoot` forever. (2) `repairDropdownAnchor` now idempotently calls `panel.showPopover()` (try/catch) after rewiring `position-anchor` — a structural `rebuildShellDOM` mints a FRESH `.tbw-tool-panel` NOT in the top layer, so re-pairing the anchor alone left it rendered only via the `.open` fallback (clip/stack risk). (3) `reanchorOpenDropdown` fallback-mode fast-path: when `!state.dropdownAnchorName` (fixed-coord mode) AND the stored trigger is still connected AND is NOT the grid host, it short-circuits ONLY when the current `.tbw-tool-panel` already has `dataset.anchor === 'below'` — a fresh rebuilt panel has no `data-anchor`, so it never wrongly skips the re-show. Avoids re-running `getBoundingClientRect` on every render/mutation while open. Tests: `shell.spec.ts` reanchor/repairDropdownAnchor suites + `ShellPlugin.spec.ts` observer-lifecycle.
- DECIDED (dropdown re-anchor after structural re-render): `ShellController.reanchorOpenDropdown()` ([shell-controller.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/shell/shell-controller.ts)) is ALSO called from `ShellPlugin.#renderShellContents` ([ShellPlugin.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/shell/ShellPlugin.ts)) inside the `if (state.isPanelOpen)` block, dropdown mode only (structural-rebuild path). WHY: `afterStructuralRender`→`rebuildShellDOM` rebuilds `.tbw-tool-panel` via `buildShellBody` EVERY structural render (column toggle/reorder), so the fresh panel is NOT in the top layer (no `showPopover`) AND the trigger node the popover anchored to may have been recreated by the same render (e.g. a consumer's custom column-header `[data-panel-toggle]` button rebuilt on a visibility toggle). Without re-anchoring the popover loses its `position-anchor` target and CSS anchor positioning collapses it to the viewport corner — looks like it "closed" but `closeToolPanel`/`hidePopover` NEVER fired (`popover-open` stays true). FIX: prefer re-pairing the existing minted `anchor-name` onto a live `[data-panel-toggle]` in place (`repairDropdownAnchor`, no popover re-show → no flash; gated by `supportsAnchorPositioning`), else fixed-coord `showToolPanelDropdown(fresh,false)`. When NO live trigger exists: a corner-origin dropdown (stored anchor === grid host) stays pinned to the corner via `showToolPanelDropdown(host,true)`; a trigger-origin dropdown whose trigger is TRANSIENTLY absent is LEFT AT ITS LAST POSITION (no corner flash) — the MutationObserver re-pairs it before paint once the outer framework re-commits the trigger. INVARIANT: a consumer custom dropdown trigger MUST carry `data-panel-toggle` to survive node recreation — an explicit `OpenToolPanelOptions.anchor` ref alone is lost once the host re-renders it (the stored ref detaches and no selector matches it). Tests: `shell.spec.ts` "reanchorOpenDropdown" (recreated trigger / surviving trigger / no-corner-flash on transient trigger loss / preserves corner-origin placement / closed + non-dropdown no-ops). happy-dom lacks Popover/anchor APIs so assertions check `state.dropdownAnchorEl`/`dataset.anchor`, not top-layer render.
- DECIDED (in-panel close button placement + mode gating): `buildShellOptions` ([shell.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/shell/shell.ts)) sets `showCloseButton` ONLY when `header.visible===false` AND `toolPanel.locked!==true` AND `mode!=='dropdown'`. WHY: the ✕ is the sole dismissal affordance when the header toggle is hidden; a locked panel can't close; dropdown ALWAYS light-dismisses (Esc/click-outside) so a ✕ is redundant clutter. PLACEMENT ([dom-builder.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/core/internal/dom-builder.ts) `buildShellBody`): single-panel ⇒ ✕ is a SIBLING of the accordion header `<button>` inside a flex `.tbw-accordion-header-row` wrapper with the `.tbw-tool-panel-close--inline` modifier (sharing the title row); multi-panel ⇒ ✕ gets its own `.tbw-tool-panel-header` row at panel top (avoids the per-accordion chevron). The accordion header is a `<button>` so the ✕ MUST NOT nest inside it; as a sibling, clicking ✕ won't toggle the accordion (`target.closest('.tbw-accordion-header')` is null). `renderShellBody` (string renderer) NEVER emits a close button — test-only/legacy; production is DOM-builder. Tests: `dom-builder.spec.ts` (inline single / header-row multi), `grid.integration.spec.ts` (dropdown omits ✕ / overlay inlines ✕).
- DECIDED (ownership inversion — two neutral hooks): (1) `processConfig(config)` (called by `ConfigManager.merge()`) — ShellPlugin folds its OWN `shellState` (light-DOM title/content, API panels) + `this.resolvedConfig` (ctor/feature config, as BASE layer) into `config.shell`; precedence light-DOM/API state > top-level `gridConfig.shell` > feature/ctor. Core config holds ZERO shell knowledge. (2) `afterStructuralRender()` (end of core `#render()`) — ShellPlugin WRAPS `.tbw-shell-body` around the existing `.tbw-grid-content` via `rebuildShellDOM` (moving the node preserves listeners + cached refs `_bodyEl`/`_headerRowEl`/`__rowsBodyEl`). Core `#render()` always passes `shellConfig=undefined` to `buildBareGridDOMIntoElement`. State self-inits in `ShellPlugin.ensureState(grid)`; torn down on disconnect via `disposeShellState()`.
- DECIDED (shell FEATURE module): `features/shell.ts` registers `registerFeature('shell', cfg => new ShellPlugin(typeof cfg==='boolean'?{}:((cfg as ShellConfig)??{})))` + augments `FeatureConfig.shell?: boolean | ShellConfig`. Subpath resolves via existing `./features/*` wildcard (NO new entry). `#render()` RE-MERGES after `#initializePlugins()`, so `processConfig` runs with the feature instance attached before `afterStructuralRender` reads `effectiveConfig.shell`. Test: `features/shell.spec.ts` "merges light-DOM shell state onto the feature instance (#370)".
- DECIDED (#370, FEATURE-INSTANCE-GATE — a feature is instantiated at most once per grid): `#initializePlugins` ([grid.ts](libs/grid/src/lib/core/grid.ts)) routes every feature-resolved plugin through `#gateFeatureInstance(fresh)`, backed by a per-grid `#featureInstances: Map<name, BaseGridPlugin>`. If the grid already has an instance for `fresh.name`, REUSE it (call `cached.refreshUserConfigFrom(fresh)` to adopt the latest `gridConfig.features.<name>` value) and DISCARD the fresh one; else cache+return fresh. The auto-register block SEEDS the map (`#featureInstances.set('shell', this.#shellPlugin)`) and, when a shell resolves from features/plugins[], repoints `this.#shellPlugin` to it. WHY: the `registerFeature('shell', cfg => new ShellPlugin(...))` factory builds a NEW instance on every re-resolution, each with its OWN `#state`. SSR/docs pattern: `<tbw-grid>` connects from server HTML before `gridConfig` is set → no `features.shell` → shell AUTO-REGISTERS (instance A, `hasState`), user `getPluginByName('shell').registerToolPanel(...)` writes API panels onto A, THEN `gridConfig.features.shell` is assigned → queued `#updatePluginConfigs` re-resolves shell from the factory → instance B with EMPTY state → A detached, panels LOST. The `#updatePluginConfigs` preserve-loop (keeps `apiToolPanelIds`/`lightDomToolPanelIds`) only worked for a SHARED single state object — silently missed the A→B instance swap. The gate makes B never exist: A is reused, its config refreshed (header title), its API panels survive. RESTORES pre-#370 behavior where shell state was a single grid-lifetime object, so deprecated `grid.registerToolPanel()` AND `getPluginByName('shell').registerToolPanel()` both work SYNCHRONOUSLY (no `grid.ready()` needed). `BaseGridPlugin.refreshUserConfigFrom(other)` = last-write-wins config replace (clears then `Object.assign` userConfig; `attach()` recomputes `config`); distinct from `mergeConfigsFrom` (alias-collapse, conflict-throwing). INVARIANT: a feature plugin keeps its identity (and accumulated state) across feature/plugin config changes for the grid's lifetime. Test: `features/shell.spec.ts` "keeps API-registered tool panels across the auto-register → feature transition (#370 regression)".
